YASS results / uc1 / uc1-edfs-s128m-pdefault-n100-rf1

uc1-edfs-s128m-pdefault-n100-rf1 edfs Success

Parameters

parametervalueunitdescription
engineedfs—Storage/transport engine under test (edfs or tus)
file_size128M—Size of the produced photo file
prioritydefault—File priority class (low/default/high; EDFS PAR)
sat_count100satsNumber of satellites in the constellation
RF1copiesEDFS replication factor (n/a for TUS)

KPIs

KPIvalueunitdescription
stateSuccess—Terminal experiment state (Success/TimedOut/Failure)
exec_date2026-06-08 02:02 UTCUTCWall-clock date/time the experiment was launched (CR creationTimestamp)
duration5m 41s—Experiment duration on the simulation clock (simulationStartTime → experimentTime)
sim_start2026-05-16 23:59 UTCUTCSimulation clock at experiment start (simulationStartTime)
sim_end2026-05-17 00:04 UTCUTCSimulation clock when the experiment finished (experimentTime)
first_GS_delivery_s154sTime from sim start until the first produced file reached any ground station
GS_with_delivery7countGround stations with a recorded delivery time
produced1filesFiles produced by the producer agent(s)
GS_reached7countGround stations that received the file (from received_total — independent of the delivery-time metric)
distinct_receivers45countDistinct nodes that received a file (GS + relays)
TX_MiB8520MiBTotal network bytes transmitted, all nodes
peak_cpu_m470millicoresPeak CPU across all containers
peak_mem_MiB229MiBPeak memory across all containers
notes——Data-quality flags for this run (missing metrics, undelivered files)

Charts (interactive)

Node colours are consistent across every chart and the graph: ■ ground station · ■ relay satellite · ■ producer.

Per-target delivery time

Seconds from file creation until each target node held the complete file. Bars coloured by node kind; the delivery KPI is the earliest ground-station bar.

Network TX over time (MiB)

Cumulative TX summed across all nodes, vs seconds from experiment start.

CPU and memory over time

Aggregate CPU (millicores, left axis) and memory (MiB, right axis) across all containers, vs seconds from experiment start.

Network TX by node (top 20)

Total bytes transmitted per node over the run, coloured by node kind.

Peak CPU per node (top 20)

Peak CPU (millicores) reached by each fsNode during the run, coloured by node kind.

Peak memory per node (top 20)

Peak memory (MiB) reached by each fsNode during the run, coloured by node kind.

File propagation graph

■ ground station · ● relay satellite · ● producer (same colours as the charts). Spread tree: one edge from → to per node = where it first acquired the file by the selected time. Grouped per file; 0-byte control messages omitted; times bucketed to ~2 s for cross-node clock skew. Timeline spans the whole experiment (0 = simulation start). Delivery completeness is the File deliveries table below.

File transfer graph (rate)

Same nodes; edge from → to = bytes transferred within the trailing window ending at the selected time (instantaneous flow). Edge colour = bandwidth (bits/s, blue = low → red = high); label shows the rate. Play to watch where traffic moves moment-to-moment. Timeline is the simulation clock (0 = simulation start), shared with the charts above.

Files

  • Kubernetes resources — manifests to reproduce this run (hardwaredefinitions, layout, experimentdefinition, experiment)
  • metrics.json — KPIs as JSON
  • raw-parquet.tar — raw metrics & events as typed, zstd-compressed Parquet (one file per series; read with pandas/polars/DuckDB)
